name: Voting Engagement
summary: Measures how varied a user’s votes are. Higher scores mean they use a wider range of options (skip and/or 1 to 10) instead of always choosing the same value. Output is between 0 and 1.
description:
### What this algorithm is
This algorithm measures how varied a voter’s choices are.

It does not judge whether votes are correct. It only checks whether the voter uses a range of options (skip and/or 1 to 10) or keeps choosing the same option.

### How to interpret the score
- A lower score means the voter almost always chooses the same option.
- A higher score means the voter spreads their votes across more options.

### Inputs (Votes CSV)
Each row is one vote. Votes are grouped by voter using collection_id.

Rows are ignored if collection_id, question_id, or answer is missing, or if answer is not one of: skip, 1, 2, ..., 10.

### Outputs
- A per-voter CSV with a score between 0 and 1.
- A JSON details file with each voter’s vote distribution and processing stats (useful for auditing and debugging).

inputs.label: Votes CSV
inputs.description: Votes input CSV that includes voter id, proposal id, and an answer value of skip or 1 to 10 (invalid rows are ignored).

columns.description: Last update timestamp of the vote
Balance at time of vote (if provided)
Stake associated with the vote (if provided)

outputs.label: Voting Engagement by Voter
outputs.description: Per-voter CSV with columns: collection_id (voter id), voting_engagement (score in [0, 1]).

outputs.label: Voting Engagement Details (JSON)
outputs.description: Per-voter breakdown including vote distribution across the 11 categories plus processing metrics (valid/invalid rows). For debugging and auditing.
